channel.title: Posts on Philipp Schmalen
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Philipp Schmalen

item.title: Predicting ESG risks with Pytorch, Google Trends and Amazon SageMaker
item.description: Predicting ESG risks with Pytorch, Google Trends and Amazon SageMaker Introduction The project deals with a prediction task within finance, and in particular, sustainable investment. Private investors increasingly demand firms that act socially responsible, environmentally friendly and have good governance. The UN and leading asset management firms developed the three pillars of ESG, namely environmental, social and governmental, in a conference in 2005 (International Finance Corporation et al., 2005). A few years ago investors were willing to accept lower returns for socially responsible investments, but firms with higher ESG scores closed the gap.

item.title: A comprehensive guide to Causal Forest
item.description: Disclaimer This guide aims to be a great resource for practitioners of causal forest and wants to empower researchers with this awesome method. I found myself diving into its R source code, published papers on its theory and referring to its algorithm reference. I want to combine all these resources in one article, so you do not need to check every resource in-detail and dig deep. Still, you are welcome to do so and I refer everything under references.

item.title: Design Recommender Systems with Recommenderlab
item.description: Build your own recommender system with Recommenderlab I show Recommenderlab’s scope to customize recommender algorithms and discuss how to compare different recommender techniques.
